nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=155 beats='{HERO} cannot sleep, so she climbs to the attic and switches on {KIN}'s old radio set; out of {NOISE} comes {SIGNAL} she heard last night, a voice reading a string of numbers, then {DEADLINE_SIGN} that resets and begins again; three threads tug at her, {ROUTE_A_CLUE} buried in the numbers, {THRESHOLD}, and {ROUTE_C_CLUE} {KIN} kept'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='{HERO} copies the numbers onto graph paper and treats them as {ROUTE_A_CLUE}; two of them resolve to places she knows, {A1_ZONE} down the hill and {A2_ZONE} on the ridge, and she has to pick which to walk to before her handheld radio's batteries run out'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='{A1_ZONE} is asleep, ropes ticking against masts; {ROUTE_A_CLUE} point between {A1_OFFER1}, where a light still burns, and {A1_OFFER2} that everyone says was shut years ago'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='{A1_LANDMARK} is layered with faded flyers, but {A1_FIND} is newer than the rest, inked by a careful hand with words claiming {SIGNAL} is real; someone has been leaving breadcrumbs for anyone who listens hard enough'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=125 beats='{A1_FIND}'s frequency matches {SIGNAL} exactly, and the ink is barely dry; {HERO} can follow the slip as the deliberate clue it plainly is, or she can decide a weathered card proves nothing and let the wind have it'>>

choices.label: Dismiss it as old junk and move on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='{HERO} tells herself a scrap of card means nothing and lets it flap loose into the dark; only later does she wonder whose careful hand wrote it, and by then {SIGNAL} is just a sound she used to chase'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{A1_HELPER} listens to {SIGNAL} on {HERO}'s phone and frowns; he says a signal like that used to run off {THRESHOLD}, and that whoever kept it going was stubborn as barnacles, then he points her toward the truth without quite meaning to'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{A1_OFFER2} is padlocked but the window is cracked; inside, {A1_DETAIL1} blinks in time with {SIGNAL}, proof it reaches here too, and {HERO} weighs forcing the warped door against simply logging the frequency and going home'>>

choices.label: Just log the frequency and leave.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The door gives with a groan; {A1_DETAIL1} is wired to a hand-drawn card reading relay {CALL_SIGN}, and {HERO} realizes this set is only an echo of something broadcasting from {THRESHOLD}'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='{HERO} writes the frequency neatly in her notebook and walks home, telling herself she will come back with a plan; she never quite does, and {SIGNAL} keeps counting down to no one, a light blinking over an empty {A1_ZONE}'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='{A2_ZONE} stands rusted against the stars, a stub of antenna still bolted to its cap; {SIGNAL} is stronger here, and {HERO} can either climb {A2_OFFER1} or search {A2_OFFER2}'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='Halfway up {A2_OFFER1} the wind shoves at {HERO}, but from the cap she sees {A2_DETAIL1} running toward the dark shape of {THRESHOLD}; {SIGNAL} is not here, it only passes through'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{A2_OFFER2} is furred with rust; one panel is loose enough to pry, though it might take her whole night, while {A2_DETAIL2} simply points along the ridge toward {THRESHOLD}'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Inside {A2_OFFER2} is a junction and a laminated tag reading {CALL_SIGN} RELAY, DO NOT DISCONNECT; {A2_ZONE} is only a repeater, and the real transmitter is further out at {THRESHOLD}'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='{A2_DETAIL2} leads {HERO} along a cracked service path toward {THRESHOLD}; whoever laid this trail wanted it followed, and {SIGNAL} grows steadier with every step'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{HERO} pieces together every clue on her graph paper; every arrow, every echo, points to one source at {THRESHOLD}, and {DEADLINE_SIGN} feels suddenly less like noise and more like a message with an address'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='The last leg of {ROUTE_A_CLUE}'s trail brings {HERO} to a wind-scoured overlook above {THRESHOLD}; {SIGNAL} is loudest here, unmistakably alive, and whatever it is, it is close enough now to touch'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='{HERO} wheels her bike toward {THRESHOLD}; it squats at the cliff edge, its mast a black needle against the moon, and two ways lead in, {B1_ZONE} or {B2_ZONE} that curves behind the compound'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{B1_ZONE} runs a hands-width from the fence above a long drop; the wind is vicious, and {HERO} spots {B1_GATE}, though part of her says to photograph the mast and retreat before she is blown off the ledge'>>

choices.label: Photograph the mast and turn back.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{HERO} squeezes through {B1_GATE} into the compound; {B1_LANDMARK} reads RELAY {CALL_SIGN}, and {B1_DETAIL1}, the same rhythm as {SIGNAL}, someone or something inside still keeping it alive'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='The gust nearly takes {HERO} off her feet, so she snaps three blurred photos and pedals home, heart hammering; the pictures show only a dark tower, and {SIGNAL} plays on without her, a mystery she can describe but never solve'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{B2_ZONE} is half under seawater but sheltered from the wind; ahead sits {B2_OFFER1} with {B2_DETAIL1} visible through the glass, while a side track detours to {B2_OFFER2} humming louder than the tide, and another path ducks into {B2_OFFER3}'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='{B2_OFFER3}'s steps drop into ankle-deep seawater and brine; taped above the waterline is a sleeve holding {B2_FIND}, and the water keeps rising with every wave outside'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=125 beats='{B2_FIND} reads a vow to keep the channel open, signed with the call sign {CALL_SIGN}; {HERO} can peel it free and pocket the proof, or she can feel {B2_GATE} climbing past her knees and scramble back before the cellar fills'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='{B2_GATE} wins the argument; {HERO} backs up the streaming steps as the cellar swallows {B2_FIND} whole, and she stands dripping with nothing to show but a glimpse of a call sign and the certainty she was one wave too slow'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{B2_OFFER1} is unlocked and warm, as if just left; {B2_DETAIL1} logs transmissions by hand, the last line only hours old, when {B2_DETAIL2} sounds somewhere in the dark and {HERO} must decide to read on or grab what she can and slip out'>>

choices.label: Grab a torn log page and slip away.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{B2_DETAIL1} is decades deep in one patient hand; the final entries repeat a single note, still no answer, and {HERO} understands {SIGNAL} is not automatic at all but tended by a real, waiting person'>>

choices.label: Carry the log toward the source.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='{HERO} pockets a torn page and ducks out; under a streetlight she reads a single line addressed to {LISTENER}, still holding, and {SIGNAL} stops being a machine and becomes a message somebody meant for someone'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{B2_OFFER2} throbs with power feeding the mast; {B2_DETAIL3} names {OPERATOR}, in handwriting {HERO} has already seen tonight on a dozen faded cards'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Standing in the dripping compound, {HERO} gathers what {THRESHOLD} has told her; {SIGNAL} is deliberate, tended, addressed, and it is counting toward something a real person set in motion and is still, somewhere, waiting on'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='{HERO} wipes the rain from her eyes and steps toward the one lit window; {SIGNAL} pulses through the wall in time with the glow, and she knows beyond this glass is the person who has been broadcasting into the dark all along'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='Back home, {HERO} hunts for {ROUTE_C_CLUE}; {KIN} listened to the airwaves for forty years and wrote it all down, and it could be in {C1_ZONE} or out in {C2_ZONE} with its wall of pinned notes'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{C1_ZONE} breathes out cedar and old paper; inside lie {C1_FIND1} and {C1_FIND2}, plus {C1_FIND3}, and {HERO} can only read one carefully before her eyes give out'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='Under the cards lies {C1_FIND3}, letters {KIN} never sent, all addressed to {THRESHOLD}; the top one begs {OPERATOR} to keep the channel open, and {HERO} realizes the two old men shared this vigil for years'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=125 beats='The letter names {OPERATOR} plainly and pleads with him not to give up on {LISTENER}; it feels like reading a stranger's heart, and {HERO} can press on because {SIGNAL} needs an answer, or she can retie the ribbon, deciding some grief is not hers to open'>>

choices.label: Feel it is too private and stop.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='{HERO} reknots the ribbon and sets the letters gently back, unwilling to pry into two old men's sorrow; it is a kind choice and a final one, for the names and frequencies she needed were folded inside, and the trail simply ends'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='{KIN} logged {SIGNAL} for years, always with the same underlined note asking why {OPERATOR} was still transmitting; the last page, dated the week he died, simply asks whether anyone will finally answer the poor man'>>

choices.label: Take up the question he left.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{C1_FIND2} are from listeners all over the coast, all logging the same stubborn signal; one card, heavier than the rest, names an exact frequency and {C1_DETAIL1}, while the others are only friendly postcards she could set aside'>>

choices.label: Set the postcards aside for now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{C1_DETAIL1} gives {HERO} a name to hang the mystery on, and a frequency matching {SIGNAL} to the decimal; whoever {OPERATOR} is, the whole coast of listeners has quietly heard him for years and no one ever answered'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='{HERO} stacks the postcards back in {C1_ZONE} and closes the lid, meaning to come back when she is less tired; morning brings school and ordinary noise, and the cards, and {SIGNAL}, slip quietly back into the past'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{C2_ZONE} smells of solder and dust; {C2_DETAIL1} are pinned in ranks across one wall, but a padlocked filing cabinet is out of reach, and {HERO} weighs reading {C2_OFFER1} against forcing {C2_OFFER2}'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='One yellowed note among {C2_DETAIL1} is circled twice, naming {THRESHOLD} and {OPERATOR}, do not give up on him; {KIN} had been trying to reach the sender for decades, and now the task, unfinished, has passed to {HERO}'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='{C2_OFFER2} will not yield and the hasp is screwed tight; without the key it keeps its secrets, and by the time {HERO} thinks to search for it the trail has gone cold, {SIGNAL} just one more thing {KIN} understood and she never will'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Everything {HERO} has found among {KIN}'s papers converges on one stubborn fact, {OPERATOR} has been transmitting from {THRESHOLD} for a lifetime, and she is holding the only thread that leads back to him'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='Papers spread across {KIN}'s desk, {HERO} traces the last line of his research to its end; there is nothing left to read and only one thing left to do, go to {THRESHOLD} and face the man {KIN} could never quite reach'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=150 beats='However she got here, {HERO} arrives at the same certainty; {SIGNAL} is no accident and no machine left running, it is the deliberate work of one person, {OPERATOR}, {THRESHOLD}'s last operator, looping the same numbers and {DEADLINE_SIGN} for years because nobody has ever answered back; the who is settled, and it only sharpens the harder questions'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{HERO} pins {OPERATOR}'s name to her corkboard beside the frequency she has traced; {SIGNAL} cycles again in her headphones, patient and sad, and two ways forward open, to crack the coded numbers themselves or to learn who {OPERATOR} really is'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The mystery has a person at its center now, but a person is not an answer; {HERO} can bend her attention to the numbers {OPERATOR} reads, hunting the code hidden in them, or she can chase {OPERATOR} himself through the town records and the people who remember him'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='{HERO} writes the number string out in full and stares; it could be {D_TOOL1} keyed to a text she already owns, {ROUTE_C_CLUE} maybe, or it could be {D_TOOL2}, ticking toward one fixed moment, and each theory needs a different tool'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Using {D1_KEY1}, {HERO} maps each figure to a word; fragments surface, {D1_FRAGMENT} and the letter {LISTENER}, enough to feel real, but one cluster stays gibberish and she must decide whether to keep forcing {D1_KEY1} or switch to {D1_KEY2}'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{D1_KEY1} holds; the numbers resolve into a plain, aching sentence, {D1_FRAGMENT} for {LISTENER}, and the final group is not a word at all but a running count, {SIGNAL} is measuring how long is left'>>

choices.label: See what the count is measuring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{D1_KEY2} is battered, and against it the stubborn cluster finally speaks, a date and an hour; but the rest turns to noise on this key, and {HERO} must judge whether she has enough or has hit a wall'>>

choices.label: Admit the cipher has beaten her.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{D1_KEY2} gives {HERO} a single decoded certainty, a date and hour only days away, wrapped around {D1_FRAGMENT} for {LISTENER}; the numbers are a clock, and the clock is nearly run down'>>

choices.label: Learn what the clock ends on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='The cipher slips through {HERO}'s fingers, every new key spitting out fresh nonsense until the numbers blur together; she closes her notebooks well past midnight, no closer to the meaning, {SIGNAL} still counting in the dark toward a total she cannot read'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Treating the string as {D_TOOL2}, {HERO} logs the intervals between broadcasts; they are shrinking, each cycle a little shorter than the last, and she can either keep {D2_METHOD1} to project its end or {D2_METHOD2} against the pattern'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The intervals are collapsing on a curve; {HERO} plots them and the line drives toward zero within the week, {SIGNAL} is not eternal after all, it is counting down, and something happens when it reaches the bottom'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Laid over a calendar the intervals lock onto one date, and beside it in {SIGNAL} sits the repeated letter {LISTENER}; {HERO} can commit to that reading and push on, or she can decide she is forcing a pattern that is not really there and stop'>>

choices.label: Commit to the date and push on.
Decide she is imagining the pattern.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{HERO} circles the date twice; {SIGNAL} is a countdown to that exact night, threaded through with a name, and whatever {OPERATOR} set in motion is meant to finish then whether anyone is listening or not'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Doubt wins; {HERO} decides she has been reading shapes into {NOISE}, the way people see faces in clouds, and she puts her charts away for good; {SIGNAL} keeps its rhythm without her, and she never learns she was right all along'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='To find {OPERATOR}, {HERO} needs someone who remembers; {E_SOURCE1} goes back a century, and {E_SOURCE2} has known that headland since before {THRESHOLD} was built'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The reader whirs; {HERO} finds {E1_FIND}, {THRESHOLD} closed, its staff let go, and a photograph of a lone technician who refused a transfer, but {E_SOURCE1} is shutting within the hour and she cannot read every reel'>>

choices.label: Photograph the pages before it closes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The article names him, {OPERATOR}, the one operator who stayed on unpaid after the shutdown, insisting {THRESHOLD} still had a job to do; the reporter thought it a sad eccentricity, but {HERO} reads {SIGNAL} underneath it now'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{HERO} shoots the pages just as the lights dim; later, zooming in, she reads the caption naming {OPERATOR}, and a margin note wondering if he ever stopped broadcasting, which tells her {SIGNAL} has outlived the whole town's memory'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{E_SOURCE2} hands {HERO} a mug of tea and studies her a long moment; she knew {OPERATOR}, she says, knew {E2_DETAIL}, then she pauses, weighing whether the girl truly wants to carry this or should be sent gently home to bed'>>

choices.label: Promise her you mean to help.
Waver, and let her send you home.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Satisfied, {E_SOURCE2} tells it plain; {OPERATOR}'s daughter left after a terrible quarrel and never wrote, and he swore he would keep a channel open for her until she answered, a promise he has kept alone for thirty years'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='{HERO} hesitates a beat too long, and {E_SOURCE2} kindly decides the past is too heavy for a school night; she walks {HERO} to the door with a pat and a thermos, and the story of {OPERATOR} stays sealed in an old woman's memory, {SIGNAL} still calling in the cold'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=150 beats='The threads knot into one answer; {SIGNAL} repeats because it is a countdown that resets every time it goes unanswered, and it is not broadcast to the world at all, it is addressed, aimed at a single listener marked only {LISTENER} in {OPERATOR}'s logs; for thirty years he has held a channel open for someone who never called back, restarting {SIGNAL} each time the silence won'>>

choices.label: Take in the weight of it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{HERO} sits back, headphones warm against her ears, {SIGNAL} suddenly unbearably human; two questions remain and they braid together, who exactly is {LISTENER}, and what happens when this newest countdown finally reaches zero'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='She cannot answer a signal she does not understand; {HERO} can hunt down who {LISTENER} really is, the one {OPERATOR} has waited a lifetime for, or she can work out exactly what the shrinking countdown is racing toward'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='{LISTENER} could be a person or a place; {HERO} can try {F_METHOD1}, or she can {F_METHOD2}, a landfall he keeps naming for a ship or a soul that never arrives'>>

choices.label: Test {LISTENER} as a place on the map.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='{F1_LEAD1} shows a daughter gone from the county three decades back; there is also {F1_LEAD2}, still local, and {HERO} must choose which lead to pin her hope on with the clock already shrinking'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='{LISTENER} is {OPERATOR}'s daughter, who walked out after their last fight and has drifted from town to town since; {SIGNAL} is his standing apology, broadcast on the frequency they once shared, waiting for the day she tunes back in'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{F1_LEAD2}, reached by phone, weeps softly; she confirms {LISTENER} is {OPERATOR}'s only child, and gives {HERO} the last address she has, a long shot two towns west, and one plea before it is too late for them both'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='On the chart, {LISTENER} could mark {F2_LANDMARK} off the headland where a boat once went down; {HERO} can chase that reading, or she can admit the map is leading her in circles while the real answer is a person, not a place'>>

choices.label: Give up the map as a dead end.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{F2_LANDMARK}'s plaque carries a name, {LISTENER}, painted the year {OPERATOR}'s daughter vanished; {LISTENER} is not a place after all but a person the whole headland has quietly mourned, and she may yet be alive to hear him'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='{HERO} chases the map in widening circles until every lead folds back on itself; exhausted, she saves a clean recording of {SIGNAL} to her phone and lets the search go, carrying a mystery she can play for people but never finish'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='To pin the countdown target, {HERO} can try {G_METHOD1}, or she can test her hunch it is timed to the coast itself, to {G2_WEATHER} swelling toward the headland this very week'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The math lands the zero point on a night only days off; but a countdown needs a thing to reach, and {HERO} must decide whether zero marks {DEADLINE} finally failing or {FAREWELL} playing automatically when the wait runs out'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{THRESHOLD} runs on {DEADLINE}, and {HERO}'s extrapolation matches its rated end almost to the hour; when it fails {SIGNAL} fails with it, and {OPERATOR}'s thirty-year channel to {LISTENER} goes dark unless someone acts first'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Buried in the logs {HERO} finds it, {FAREWELL}, {OPERATOR}'s goodbye to {LISTENER}, set to speak once into the dark and be gone'>>

choices.label: Resolve to catch it before it erases.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The countdown does align with {G2_WEATHER} forecast for the headland; that could mean the storm will simply swamp {THRESHOLD}, and {HERO} can raise the alarm or hold back, unsure she has the story right'>>

choices.label: Warn someone about {G2_WEATHER} and the relay.
Hold back until she is certain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='{HERO} connects it, {G2_WEATHER} will swamp the headland the same night {DEADLINE} fails, ending {SIGNAL} twice over; the countdown is real and physical now, weather and machinery closing on one hour, and the window to act is almost shut'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='{HERO} decides she needs to be sure before she troubles anyone, so she waits one more night to double-check her figures; in the morning {SIGNAL} is gone, only clean {NOISE} where the countdown used to be, {THRESHOLD} silenced while she hesitated'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=155 beats='Everything {HERO} has gathered snaps into one clear, ticking shape; the countdown ends this coming night, when {DEADLINE} finally fails, and the moment it does {FAREWELL} will play, {OPERATOR}'s recorded farewell to the one he has waited so long to reach, and then erase itself into silence with {G2_WEATHER} rolling in behind it; thirty years of waiting come down to one hour, and {HERO} is the only person who knows'>>

choices.label: Decide how to spend that hour.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Rain begins to freckle the window as {HERO} weighs her options; {THRESHOLD} is a bike ride away, {KIN}'s transmitter still works, and the town has people who could move faster than she can, but every path runs against the same shrinking clock'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='One hour, three ways to spend it; {HERO} can race to {THRESHOLD} itself and answer {SIGNAL} at its source, she can use {KIN}'s rig to rebroadcast {FAREWELL} wide enough that {LISTENER} might hear it, or she can rouse people who could reach the headland faster and surer than a girl on a bike in a gale'>>

choices.label: Get help from people who can reach it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{HERO} pedals into {G2_WEATHER} and reaches {H_ENTRY}; the mast light pulses with {SIGNAL} above her, minutes left on the clock, and she can go over the rattling fence into {H1_ZONE} or gamble on {H2_ZONE}'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{HERO} tops the fence and drops into {H1_ZONE}; {H1_ROOM}'s door hangs ajar ahead, spilling faint light, but the exposed climb has cost her precious minutes and {H1_GATE} is trying to peel her off the concrete, so she can push straight for that door or catch her breath first'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=145 beats='Inside, {H1_ROOM} is a shrine of dials and one glowing console counting down in red; {HERO} can seize {H1_CHOICE1} and answer the transmitter directly, or she can see {DEADLINE} guttering and try to stabilize it before it fails mid-message'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=145 beats='The countdown reads under a minute; {HERO} finds {OPERATOR}'s contact list open on the desk, {H1_CONTACT} never dialed, and she can bridge that line and let {FAREWELL} finally reach a living ear, or simply key the transmitter to halt the wipe and save {H1_ARCHIVE}'>>

choices.label: Bridge the line to {LISTENER} and play it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='{HERO} dials {H1_CONTACT} and patches it through as the counter hits zero; across three towns a phone rings, a startled woman answers, and her father's recorded voice, thirty years patient, fills the line, still holding the channel open for {LISTENER}, and this time somebody is there to hear it'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='{HERO} keys the override a breath before the wipe; the countdown freezes, {SIGNAL} steadies, and thirty years of {OPERATOR}'s logs and messages are saved intact, {H1_ARCHIVE} preserved so that when {LISTENER} is found, every word he sent will still be waiting for her'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{DEADLINE} is a corroded brick barely holding voltage; {HERO} remembers {KIN}'s spare battery in her pack and can splice it in to buy {THRESHOLD} hours and then call the whole thing in properly, or she can nurse it by hand and pray it lasts through one last broadcast'>>

choices.label: Splice in the spare and call it in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='The spliced cell catches and the console steadies; {HERO} gets {THRESHOLD} stable, then phones {I_GROUP} with the whole story, and by dawn {OPERATOR}'s loop is safe on backup power while volunteers begin the search for {LISTENER}, the countdown stopped and the waiting handed on to people who can finish it'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='{HERO}'s hands are steady but {DEADLINE} is too far gone; it fails with a small final click just as {FAREWELL} begins, cutting {OPERATOR}'s farewell to a single word, {LISTENER}, before the room goes dark and silent, though {HERO}'s clean recording of that last word is all that survives the night'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Pinned by {H1_GATE}, {HERO} loses her nerve and her seconds together; by the time she forces herself to the door the console has already gone dark, the countdown spent, and she is left in a dead {H1_ROOM} with a mystery solved one minute too late'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{H2_ZONE} is longer but sheltered, and {HERO} pedals hard around to a back door standing open; a lamp burns inside, and there, hunched over the console he has tended for thirty years, is {OPERATOR} himself, or she finds {H2_GATE} after all and time bleeding away'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=145 beats='{OPERATOR} turns, startled to find a girl dripping in his doorway; {HERO} blurts everything she has pieced together, {SIGNAL}, the countdown, {LISTENER}, and she can beg him to let {FAREWELL} go out live tonight while there is still power, or watch him shake his head, certain after thirty years no one is really listening'>>

choices.label: Fail to move him, and leave a note.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='Something in {HERO}'s certainty reaches the old man; {OPERATOR} wipes his eyes, leans to {H1_CHOICE1}, and instead of the recording speaks live for the first time in decades, telling the open channel he is still here, still sorry, still waiting, and two towns west {LISTENER}, who never stopped listening, finally lifts the handset'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='{OPERATOR} only shakes his head; thirty years of silence have convinced him no answer is coming, and he will not risk hoping again tonight, not even for a stranger. {HERO} leaves her notebook of everything she found on his desk and bikes home in the rain, the choice, and {SIGNAL}, left in his tired hands'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='{H2_GATE} is bolted and no knock brings anyone; {HERO} huddles under the eave counting the minutes until the mast light stutters and goes still, the countdown finished on the far side of a locked door, so close she could hear the last tone fade through the wall'>>

ending.title: On the Wrong Side of the Door

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{HERO} stays home and runs for the attic instead; if she cannot reach {THRESHOLD} in time, she can amplify {FAREWELL} so far and wide that {LISTENER} might stumble on it, using {KIN}'s own transmitter, or she can wake {I_GROUP} to throw it across the whole county net'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{KIN}'s rig hums to life under {HERO}'s hands, warm and familiar; she can boost {SIGNAL} and splice {LISTENER}'s name across every band she can legally reach, praying it carries, or she can push the old set past its limits for more range than it was ever built to give'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{HERO} rebroadcasts {FAREWELL} on a steady boost, {LISTENER}'s name riding {SIGNAL} out across the dark coast; for a long minute there is only {NOISE}, and then she must decide whether the faint carrier swelling back is truly an answer or just her own hope, and whether to keep calling'>>

choices.label: Trust a neighbor heard, and stop.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='The carrier resolves into a voice, hesitant and disbelieving, asking who is broadcasting her name; {HERO} explains in a rush, her father, {THRESHOLD}, thirty years, and hands the channel to {SIGNAL}, and {LISTENER} listens to her father's waiting message with a stranger girl as witness, the silence finally, gently broken'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='No answer comes for {LISTENER}, but a neighbor two streets over hears the whole broadcast and calls, shaken, promising to spread it through every relay and club she knows; {HERO} has not closed {SIGNAL} herself, yet she has thrown {FAREWELL} far past {THRESHOLD}, and now a hundred listeners will carry it on'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='{HERO} pushes the old rig too hard and it dies in a wisp of blue smoke and the smell of scorched solder; the attic goes quiet, {KIN}'s transmitter finished the same night as {OPERATOR}'s loop, and she is left with a dark set and a message she could not send'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{HERO} rings {I_GROUP}'s emergency line; {I2_CONTACT} answers, then wakes fully as the story spills out, and he can throw {FAREWELL} onto the county-wide net at once for maximum reach, or she can hear that no one is on shift and only a recorder is listening at this hour'>>

choices.label: Push it onto the county net now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{I_GROUP} fires {FAREWELL} across the whole county net, dozens of stations relaying {LISTENER}'s name into {G2_WEATHER}; within minutes reports trickle back, and {HERO} must judge whether {I2_LEAD} is the lead she has prayed for or a coincidence to chase'>>

choices.label: Chase the lead two ridges west.
Log every lead for {I_GROUP} to follow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='The lead is real; the woman two ridges west is {LISTENER}, up late and idly scanning the bands she abandoned decades ago, and the sound of her own name in her father's old loop stops her cold. {I_GROUP} patches her through to {THRESHOLD}, and {OPERATOR}'s thirty-year wait ends in a single trembling hello'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='{HERO} cannot be sure any single lead is {LISTENER}, so she logs them all and hands {I_GROUP} a complete file, the frequency, the story, every promising callback; the net keeps {FAREWELL} circulating night after night, and though {HERO} does not see the ending herself, {OPERATOR}'s loop is finally in a hundred searching hands'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Only the after-hours recorder answers, and {HERO} leaves the whole story into the void, hoping someone plays it back at dawn; but dawn is after the countdown, and by the time a club volunteer hears her message {THRESHOLD}'s loop has already fallen silent, her call arriving a night too late'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{HERO} decides she cannot do this alone against the clock; she can phone {J_METHOD1}, who treat any headland beacon seriously and can reach {THRESHOLD} by road in minutes, or she can track down {J_METHOD2}, a man who would understand every word of what she has found'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{J1_HELPER} takes {HERO} seriously the moment she says a beacon on the headland is counting down in {G2_WEATHER}; he can dispatch a crew to {THRESHOLD} to secure it as a live signal, or he can hesitate, unable to log a mystery loop as a real emergency without more than a schoolgirl's word'>>

choices.label: Convince him to dispatch a crew.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='A crew rolls for the headland with {HERO} guiding them by phone; reaching {THRESHOLD} ahead of {G2_WEATHER}, they radio back that the console is alive and counting, and {HERO} must tell them what matters most in the last minute, to trace {FAREWELL}'s intended listener or to preserve the failing station whole'>>

<<FILL role=success words=130 beats='The crew patches {HERO} straight into {THRESHOLD}, and with their gear behind {OPERATOR}'s thirty-year loop the signal leaps its old limits; they trace the return call to {LISTENER} within the hour and hold the channel open until father and daughter are, at last and across all that silence, speaking to each other'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='The crew secures {THRESHOLD} minutes before {G2_WEATHER} hits, swapping {DEADLINE} and locking {SIGNAL} safely onto backup power; nothing is lost, and with the station stable {J_METHOD1} opens a proper search for {LISTENER}, crediting the whole discovery to the girl who would not let a strange signal go'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Without a real emergency to log, {J1_HELPER} thanks {HERO} politely and files her call under curiosities; no crew rolls, and out on the headland the countdown runs down unwatched, {SIGNAL} dying in {G2_WEATHER} while the one report that could have saved it sits closed in a duty logbook'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{HERO} tracks down {J2_HELPER}; he goes quiet when she describes {SIGNAL}, then says he always feared {OPERATOR} would do this, and he can drive out to help her reach {THRESHOLD} in time, or she can learn his number is years dead and the man long moved away'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='{J2_HELPER} meets {HERO} at {H_ENTRY} with keys he never returned; he knows the console cold, and together they can complete {OPERATOR}'s scheduled transmission the right way before {DEADLINE} fails, or {HERO} can step back and let the old professional take it from here, her part in the search already done'>>

choices.label: Step back and let {J2_HELPER} finish it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='Side by side, {HERO} and {J2_HELPER} bring {THRESHOLD} to full power and send {FAREWELL} out clean and strong on the exact frequency {LISTENER} once knew; when the answer comes, faint then sure, {J2_HELPER} grips {HERO}'s shoulder, and {SIGNAL}, which counted down alone for thirty years, ends with two people there to hear it land'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='{HERO} hands {J2_HELPER} the story and steps back, and he takes over with the ease of a man who built the place; she bikes home certain it is in good hands, yet she never hears how it ends, whether {LISTENER} answered or {SIGNAL} simply stopped, her long night closing on a question she will always carry'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='The number rings out to a stranger; {J2_HELPER} moved away years ago and left no forwarding line, and every other name from the relay days has scattered or passed on. {HERO} sits with the dead phone as the clock runs down, the one person who could have helped her already gone beyond reach'>>
